problem_4.natural_language: If `f^n(v) ≠ 0` and `f^(n+1)(v) = 0`, then `v, f v, …, f^n v` are linearly independent.
problem_4.lean:
theorem linearIndependent_iterates_of_pow_succ_eq_zero
    {K V : Type*} [DivisionRing K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) (v : V) (n : ℕ)
    (hn : (f ^ n) v ≠ 0) (hn1 : (f ^ (n + 1)) v = 0) :
    LinearIndependent K (fun i : Fin (n + 1) => (f ^ (i : ℕ)) v)
problem_4.natural_language_proof:
Proof: suppose we have a linear relation
`a0 • v + a1 • (f v) + ... + an • (f ^ n v) = 0`.
Apply `f ^ n` to this equality. All terms with `i ≥ 1` become `f ^ (n+i) v = 0` since `n+i ≥ n+1`,
while the first term becomes `a0 • (f ^ n v)`. Hence `a0 • (f ^ n v) = 0`, and since `f ^ n v ≠ 0`
we get `a0 = 0`. Now apply the same argument to the remaining relation: apply `f ^ (n-1)` to deduce
`a1 = 0`, and so on. Iterating, all coefficients are zero, so the family
`v, f v, …, f ^ n v` is linearly independent.
